data.description:
## Problem

c11 workspaces can already carry a custom color, but individual surface tabs inside panes cannot. Operators can color a workspace in the sidebar and theme chrome around that workspace, but they cannot mark the terminal/browser/markdown surfaces inside a pane with distinct colors. In dense multi-agent workspaces, pane tabs need their own lightweight identity markers so a user can distinguish roles, tasks, or risk levels without renaming every tab.

## Current architecture notes

- Workspace custom color lives on `Workspace.customColor`, mutates through `Workspace.setCustomColor(_:)`, persists as `SessionWorkspaceSnapshot.customColor`, and is exposed over `workspace.set_custom_color` / `workspace.list`.
- Pane tabs are Bonsplit `TabItem`s keyed by surface/panel UUIDs. c11 keeps panel-owned state in `Workspace` dictionaries keyed by panel ID (`panelCustomTitles`, `pinnedPanelIds`, unread state, per-panel git/PR/ports, etc.) and mirrors renderable state into Bonsplit through `BonsplitController.updateTab`.
- Workspace color palette and custom color normalization/display helpers already exist as `WorkspaceTabColorSettings`; despite the name, they currently serve workspace colors.
- Surface metadata is a separate manifest contract for agents/Lattice. It may expose tab color later, but tab color should be a first-class c11 UI preference, not only opaque metadata.

## Desired direction

Add per-surface/pane-tab custom colors. A tab color belongs to the surface/panel identity and follows that surface when it is reordered, moved between panes, or detached/attached across workspaces/windows. It should render in the Bonsplit tab strip without changing Ghostty terminal content, browser page content, or the workspace-level custom color.

## Scope

- Add a nullable custom color field for individual surface tabs, normalized as `#RRGGBB`.
- Persist tab colors in session snapshots and restore them across app restart.
- Preserve tab colors across same-workspace moves, cross-pane moves, detach/attach, and cross-workspace/window surface moves.
- Add tab context-menu affordances for setting, choosing custom, and clearing a tab color.
- Reuse or deliberately rename/generalize the existing workspace color palette/settings helper so workspace and tab color UX share a palette without confusing code or user-facing copy.
- Render a compact color indicator in the Bonsplit tab strip for selected and unselected tabs, including small/narrow tab widths.
- Expose tab color through socket/CLI surface APIs so agents and scripts can set/clear/read it without stealing macOS focus.
- Localize all user-facing strings.

## Out of scope

- Changing workspace color behavior or fixing workspace color chrome bugs beyond seams needed to coexist cleanly.
- Tinting terminal cells, browser pages, markdown content, or Ghostty internals.
- Adding full theme-token authoring for per-tab colors unless the implementation needs a narrow theme role for contrast.
- Making Lattice assign colors automatically; this ticket only provides the c11 primitive.

## Acceptance criteria

- A user can set, clear, and choose a custom color for an individual pane tab from the tab context menu.
- The selected tab and inactive tabs show the tab color in a legible, restrained way that does not obscure favicon/loading/pin/unread/dirty/shortcut states.
- Tab color follows the surface when tabs are reordered, moved to another pane, detached, or moved to another workspace/window.
- Tab color persists across session save/restore.
- CLI/socket support can set, clear, and list/read a surface tab color by surface ref.
- Workspace color and tab color can coexist: workspace color continues to drive workspace/sidebar/theme chrome; tab color only identifies the individual surface tab.
- All new UI strings are localized in `Resources/Localizable.xcstrings`.
- Tests cover color normalization/state mutation/persistence and Bonsplit tab model propagation through runtime seams, not source-text assertions.

## Validation

Use unit tests for pure state and snapshot behavior. Use a tagged c11 build for visual validation of terminal/browser/markdown tabs across light/dark themes, workspace custom colors, pinned tabs, unread badges, loading browser favicons, and narrow split panes.
data.title: Per-surface tab colors inside panes

provenance.reason: planning references for per-surface tab color implementation
Open questions resolved: socket-only (no metadata mirror), uncolored default for new tabs, duplicate-browser-tab explicitly out of scope, same indicator for pinned tabs. Ready for implementation.

data.body: Plan open questions resolved (operator call 2026-05-04). See .lattice/plans/task_01KPSV8EP543P5RTGRJDQ7DFB6.md 'Resolved Decisions' section: socket-only (no metadata mirror), uncolored default, no duplicate-browser-tab in this ticket, same pinned-tab indicator. Bumping to planned.

Orchestration started. Orchestrator: surface:33 (pane:4). Delegator: pane:28 / surface:39. Worktree: /Users/atin/Projects/Stage11/code/c11-worktrees/c11-10-surface-tab-colors (branch c11-10-surface-tab-colors). Parent repo (canonical Lattice store): /Users/atin/Projects/Stage11/code/c11. Prompt at .lattice/prompts/c11-10-delegator.md inside the worktree. Plan is operator-approved as of 2026-05-04 (Resolved Decisions section in the plan); delegator skips fresh planning, runs Plan-verify -> Impl -> Translator -> Review -> Validate -> PR. Phase sub-agents will be sibling surfaces inside pane:28.

Delegator online (agent:claude-opus-4-7-c11-10) in surface:39. Plan-verify phase started in sibling surface:40 (tab "C11-10 Delegator :: Plan-verify"). Plan stays approved; sanity-check only. Phase order: Plan-verify -> Impl -> Translator -> Review -> Validate -> Handoff.

Plan-verify complete by agent:claude-opus-4-7-c11-10-plan-verify.

Findings (per linked area):

- Sources/Workspace.swift: matches plan. `customColor` (5077) and `setCustomColor` (6225, normalizes via `WorkspaceTabColorSettings.normalizedHex`) intact. Panel-scoped state pattern (`panelCustomTitles` 5189, `pinnedPanelIds` 5196) is the right template for `panelCustomColors`. `DetachedSurfaceTransfer` (5758) already carries `customTitle`/`isPinned`/`manuallyUnread` — adding `customColor` fits the pattern.
- Sources/TabManager.swift: matches plan. `WorkspaceTabColorSettings` lives at line 245 (not Workspace.swift; adjacent module). Current call sites: `Workspace.setCustomColor` (normalize), c11App.swift settings UI, ContentView.swift sidebar workspace tab rendering, WorkspaceLayoutExecutor. Sharing it for surface tab colors (palette + normalization) is realistic; the helper is already split between palette and normalize roles.
- Sources/SessionPersistence.swift: matches plan. `SessionPanelSnapshot` (339) has all panel-scoped fields. Adding `customColor: String?` is backwards-compatible with existing snapshots. Restore path is `Workspace.applySessionPanelMetadata` (827).
- Sources/TerminalController.swift / ContentView.swift / TabManager.swift: matches plan. `bonsplitController.updateTab` mirror seam used by Workspace at 9+ sites (title, isPinned, badges) — adding a color parameter is a natural extension.
- vendor/bonsplit Tab.swift + TabItem.swift: matches plan, with caveat. Public `Tab` and internal `TabItem` shapes are exactly as the plan describes; `TabItem` Codable uses `decodeIfPresent` defaults so adding optional `customColorHex` round-trips. `TabTransferData` wraps `TabItem` — adding the field there auto-preserves color across drag/drop, which covers the cross-pane move requirement without extra plumbing. **Caveat: vendor/bonsplit submodule is NOT initialized in this worktree** (`git submodule status` shows leading `-`, dir is empty). I verified Bonsplit files from the parent repo (/Users/atin/Projects/Stage11/code/c11/vendor/bonsplit/). Impl must run `git submodule update --init --recursive` before touching Bonsplit.
- vendor/bonsplit BonsplitController.swift: matches plan. `createTab` (86) accepts a flat parameter list — adding `customColorHex: String? = nil` is mechanical. `updateTab` (201) uses the `String?? = nil` double-optional convention for nullable fields with keep/set/clear semantics; `customColorHex: String?? = nil` fits.
- vendor/bonsplit TabItemView.swift: render seam matches plan. Has a `tabBackground` ZStack with a "Top accent indicator for selected tab" comment around line 548 — that's exactly where the plan's accent rail/dot belongs.
- CLI/c11.swift + TerminalController v2 dispatch: matches plan. `workspace-color set/clear/get/list-palette` CLI (2834) routes to `workspace.set_custom_color` v2 handler (TerminalController 2231 → 4118). Handler accepts `workspace_id` + (`hex` or `clear: true`); `surface.set_custom_color` symmetric shape is straightforward. Focus policy: handler runs through `v2MainSync` for state mutation but does NOT activate the app or steal focus — non-focus by default, matches CLAUDE.md "Socket focus policy" section (unchanged).
- Resources/Localizable.xcstrings: exists (1.4 MB). Existing `alert.customColor.*` keys in ContentView provide a reuse template for the new tab color prompt strings.
- Tests: c11Tests/WorkspaceUnitTests.swift (78 KB) and c11Tests/SessionPersistenceTests.swift (43 KB) exist and are the right homes. vendor/bonsplit/Tests/BonsplitTests/BonsplitTests.swift (57 KB) verified in parent repo.

Typing-latency note for TabItemView (clarifying the prompt):
- The c11 CLAUDE.md typing-latency hot path is `TabItemView` in **Sources/ContentView.swift** (line 10914, `struct TabItemView: View, Equatable` with custom `==` and `.equatable()` at the ForEach call site, line 8491). That's the **sidebar workspace tab**, already wired to `tab.customColor` (the workspace-level color).
- Bonsplit's `vendor/bonsplit/Sources/Bonsplit/Internal/Views/TabItemView.swift` (line 90, `struct TabItemView: View`) is a **different file with the same name**. It is NOT `Equatable`, has no `.equatable()` modifier, and re-evaluates body on tab data changes by default. Adding a `customColorHex` field to the Bonsplit TabItemView's `let` parameter list does NOT need to thread through an `==` function — that constraint is c11-specific.
- Implication for Impl: the plan's surface-tab-color rendering in Bonsplit's TabItemView is a normal SwiftUI parameter add. Don't search for an `Equatable` conformance to extend in Bonsplit; it isn't there. The c11 sidebar TabItemView already handles workspace color (out of this ticket's scope).

Refactors landed since plan was written: spot-checked recent commits (a0672b90, c5ccb3c2, e164a71e, cb42ec10, cbae93d3) — no structural changes to Workspace panel-state plumbing, BonsplitController.updateTab seam, SessionPanelSnapshot, or v2 set_custom_color handler. Plan seams remain valid.

Verdict: proceed-as-planned. Two items for Impl: (1) initialize Bonsplit submodule in the worktree before Bonsplit work; (2) ignore the prompt's claim that Bonsplit TabItemView uses `Equatable`+`.equatable()` — that constraint is on c11's ContentView.swift TabItemView and does not apply to the pane-tab render seam.

Plan-verify complete (verdict: proceed-as-planned, two corrections folded into Impl prompt). Status bumped to in_progress. Impl phase started in sibling surface:42 (tab "C11-10 Delegator :: Impl"). Bonsplit submodule remotes verified: vendor/bonsplit -> Stage-11-Agentics/bonsplit (safe — never manaflow-ai). Impl will run 6 slices commit-by-commit and post per-slice comments.

Slice 1 committed: 11cb128f `C11-10 slice 1: panel custom color state + session snapshot round-trip`. Files: Sources/Workspace.swift, Sources/SessionPersistence.swift, c11Tests/WorkspaceUnitTests.swift, c11Tests/SessionPersistenceTests.swift. Diff stats: +217/-0. Adds panelCustomColors dict, setPanelCustomColor/panelCustomColor accessors, SessionPanelSnapshot.customColor (backcompat-safe), DetachedSurfaceTransfer.customColor (cross-workspace move), and all cleanup-path wiring. Pushed origin/c11-10-surface-tab-colors. Next: slice 2 (Bonsplit data plumbing).

Slice 2 committed in three pieces:
- Bonsplit e08fc070 `Add optional customColorHex to TabItem/Tab and createTab/updateTab` (Stage-11-Agentics/bonsplit, branch c11-10-surface-tab-color, main advanced + ancestry verified). Files: TabItem.swift, Tab.swift, BonsplitController.swift, BonsplitTests.swift. +119/-5.
- c11 5b6e55b9 `C11-10 slice 2 (bonsplit pointer): TabItem.customColorHex plumbing` (vendor/bonsplit pointer bump, separate commit per submodule discipline).
- c11 f1c6ee8c `C11-10 slice 2: mirror panel custom color into Bonsplit on create/update`. setPanelCustomColor propagates via updateTab(.some(next)); attachDetachedSurface threads customColorHex through createTab. Same-workspace cross-pane moves auto-preserve via TabItem Codable. New tabs intentionally uncolored per resolved decision #2. +4/-0.

Pushed origin/c11-10-surface-tab-colors. Next: slice 3 (Bonsplit rendering — accent rail/dot at the existing render seam).

Slice 3 committed:
- Bonsplit c71ff967 `Render optional customColorHex as restrained tab accent indicator` (Stage-11-Agentics/bonsplit, main advanced + ancestry verified). Files: TabItemView.swift, TabBarColors.swift. +17/-3. Selected tab: full-height rail in customColor; unselected with customColor: same rail at 0.85 opacity; pinned tabs render same indicator. NSColor(bonsplitHex:) made internal so the renderer can reuse the existing parser without duplicating code.
- c11 b18fd841 `C11-10 slice 3: bump bonsplit pointer for tab color rendering`.

No render-level pixel tests added — pixel assertions on a 2-3pt rail are brittle, and the data path is already covered. Visual validation belongs in slice 6 / Validate phase with a tagged build.

Pushed origin/c11-10-surface-tab-colors. Next: slice 4 (UI controls — Tab Color submenu in Bonsplit + c11 callbacks + English strings).

Slice 4 committed in three pieces:
- Bonsplit 688e8fb9 `Add Tab Color submenu (clear/choose custom/palette) with localized strings`. New `TabContextAction.clearColor` / `.chooseCustomColor`; `BonsplitTabColorMenuItem` public struct; `tabColorPalette` host property; `requestSetTabColor` controller method + `didSelectTabColorPaletteEntry` delegate. New en strings (`command.tabColor.title/clearColor/chooseCustom`) via Bundle.module. ja falls back to English defaults until a Bonsplit-side translation pass.
- c11 47e19309 `C11-10 slice 4 (bonsplit pointer)`.
- c11 ab2f9dae `C11-10 slice 4: tab color context menu wired to setPanelCustomColor`. `bonsplitTabColorPalette()` snapshots WorkspaceTabColorSettings palette; clearColor → setPanelCustomColor(nil); chooseCustomColor → NSAlert hex input → setPanelCustomColor(normalized). Refreshes the bonsplit palette after a new custom color is added so it appears immediately. New English strings in Localizable.xcstrings (alert.tabColor.title/message/apply/cancel).

Deviation: chose NSAlert (parity with existing workspace-color flow) over the PaneInteractionFeatureFlag-gated presentTextInput path. Both already coexist in c11; slice 4 takes the simpler synchronous path.

Pushed origin/c11-10-surface-tab-colors. Next: slice 5 (socket + CLI surface-color).

Slice 5 committed: dc5dd408 `C11-10 slice 5: surface.set_custom_color v2 method + c11 surface-color CLI`. Files: Sources/TerminalController.swift, CLI/c11.swift. Diff stats: +163/-2.

Socket: new v2 `surface.set_custom_color` (surface_id + hex|clear=true), validates hex via WorkspaceTabColorSettings.normalizedHex before applying, runs through v2MainSync for state mutation, no focus side effects (matches CLAUDE.md socket-focus policy). `surface.list` and `surface.current` payloads now include `custom_color: String?`.

CLI: new `c11 surface-color {set|clear|get|list-palette}` mirroring workspace-color shape; auto-falls-back to CMUX_SURFACE_ID, help text disambiguates from workspace tabs. list-palette shares the workspace-color palette by design.

Pushed origin/c11-10-surface-tab-colors. Next: slice 6 (validation tests). Slices 1 and 2 already covered the bulk of test surface area; slice 6 adds Bonsplit-side coverage if needed and finalizes.

Slice 6 committed: f1cb44d6 `C11-10 slice 6: tab color tests (Bonsplit mirror + cross-workspace move)`. Files: c11Tests/WorkspaceUnitTests.swift. Diff stats: +73/-0.

Most test surface was already covered inline in slices 1 (normalization, setter, clear, teardown, SessionPanelSnapshot Codable backcompat) and 2 (Bonsplit TabItem Codable round-trip + updateTab keep/set/clear). Slice 6 fills the remaining c11-side integration gaps:
- testSetPanelCustomColorPropagatesToBonsplitTab — verifies the Workspace ↔ Bonsplit bridge (setPanelCustomColor is reflected on bonsplitController.tab(tabId).customColorHex; clear path also covered).
- testDetachAttachAcrossWorkspacesPreservesPanelCustomColor — full detach/attach cycle: source drops entry, transfer carries color, destination restores both panelCustomColor and the Bonsplit tab. Mirrors existing testDetachAttachAcrossWorkspacesPreservesNonCustomPanelTitle pattern.

No socket-layer test added — surface.set_custom_color is a thin dispatch wrapper, and CLAUDE.md discourages tests that only exercise glue. Visual / context-menu / narrow-tab validation deferred to the Validate phase with a tagged build.

Pushed origin/c11-10-surface-tab-colors. All 6 slices land cleanly. Posting Impl-complete summary next.

Impl complete by agent:claude-opus-4-7-c11-10-impl.

Commits (chronological, c11 branch c11-10-surface-tab-colors):
- 11cb128f slice 1: panel custom color state + session snapshot round-trip (model, persistence, transfer, tests)
- 5b6e55b9 slice 2 (bonsplit pointer): TabItem.customColorHex plumbing
- f1c6ee8c slice 2 (c11): mirror panel custom color into Bonsplit on create/update
- b18fd841 slice 3 (bonsplit pointer): tab color rendering
- 47e19309 slice 4 (bonsplit pointer): Tab Color submenu + palette plumbing
- ab2f9dae slice 4 (c11): tab color context menu wired to setPanelCustomColor
- dc5dd408 slice 5: surface.set_custom_color v2 method + c11 surface-color CLI
- f1cb44d6 slice 6: tab color tests (Bonsplit mirror + cross-workspace move)

Bonsplit branch (Stage-11-Agentics/bonsplit, c11-10-surface-tab-color, main advanced + ancestry verified at every bump):
- e08fc07 Add optional customColorHex to TabItem/Tab and createTab/updateTab
- c71ff96 Render optional customColorHex as restrained tab accent indicator
- 688e8fb Add Tab Color submenu (clear/choose custom/palette) with localized strings

Deviations from plan:
- Slice 4 chose NSAlert (parity with workspace customColor flow) over the PaneInteractionFeatureFlag-gated presentTextInput path. Both already coexist in c11; slice 4 takes the simpler synchronous path. Documented in commit message.
- Slice 4 c11 sets bonsplitController.tabColorPalette once at workspace init and refreshes after a custom color is added in the prompt flow; runtime refresh on UserDefaults change is deferred (no observer added). Newly-added customs from elsewhere won't appear in the menu until next launch — acceptable for v1, can be added later if visual validation finds it confusing.
- Slice 4 added Bonsplit en strings only (`command.tabColor.title/clearColor/chooseCustom`). ja.lproj falls back to English defaults via Bundle.module's value parameter until a Bonsplit-side translation pass.
- Slice 4 added c11 English strings only. Translator phase fills ja/uk/ko/zh-Hans/zh-Hant/ru.

New localized strings (English only; Translator phase fills ja/uk/ko/zh-Hans/zh-Hant/ru):
- alert.tabColor.title  = 'Custom Tab Color'
- alert.tabColor.message = 'Enter a hex color in the format #RRGGBB.'
- alert.tabColor.apply  = 'Apply'
- alert.tabColor.cancel = 'Cancel'

(Bonsplit-side strings live in vendor/bonsplit/Sources/Bonsplit/Resources/en.lproj/Localizable.strings, not c11's xcstrings:)
- command.tabColor.title = 'Tab Color'
- command.tabColor.clearColor = 'Clear Color'
- command.tabColor.chooseCustom = 'Choose Custom Color…'

Branch pushed: c11-10-surface-tab-colors. Bonsplit branch: c11-10-surface-tab-color (Stage-11-Agentics/bonsplit, main advanced, ancestry verified). PR not opened — Handoff phase owns that.

Ready for Translator + Review.

Impl complete (status=review). All 8 c11 commits + 3 Bonsplit commits pushed; PR not opened (Handoff phase). 3 documented deviations (NSAlert vs presentTextInput, palette refresh on prompt-add only, en-only strings). Translator phase started in sibling surface:43 to fill 7 new strings (4 c11 alert.tabColor.* + 3 Bonsplit command.tabColor.*) into ja/uk/ko/zh-Hans/zh-Hant/ru. After Translator, Review (trident) -> Validate -> Handoff.

Translator complete by agent:claude-opus-4-7-c11-10-translator.

c11 commit: 457f7d5b — alert.tabColor.{title,message,apply,cancel} localized for ja/uk/ko/zh-Hans/zh-Hant/ru.
Bonsplit commit: 97b18915 — command.tabColor.{title,clearColor,chooseCustom} localized; added 5 new .lproj dirs (uk, ko, zh-Hans, zh-Hant, ru) plus appended to existing ja.lproj. Branch c11-10-surface-tab-color pushed; merged to main with rebase over upstream commit 8db34f0 (chrome-scale knobs PR #4) which had landed concurrently.
Bonsplit pointer bump: 29ba7316 — submodule rolls 688e8fb9 → 97b18915 (includes my translation commit + the chrome-scale knobs PR that landed on Bonsplit main between Impl and Translator).

Locales filled: ja, uk, ko, zh-Hans, zh-Hant, ru. Branch pushed.

Notes for Review:
- Reused 'Apply' / 'Cancel' / hex-color message translations verbatim from adjacent alert.customColor.* keys for consistency.
- 'Custom Tab Color' parallels existing 'Custom Workspace Color' translations; 'tab' terminology matches command.clearTabName.title across locales.
- Bonsplit ja.lproj already existed with 2 move-tab keys (untranslated tab-color set was missing); appended the 3 tab-color keys.
- Bonsplit uk/ko/zh-Hans/zh-Hant/ru .lproj dirs created fresh containing only the 3 tab-color keys (per scope guardrail: don't translate keys not in the impl set, leaving the older move-tab keys English-fallback in those locales — separate scope).

Ready for Review.

Translator complete (10 c11 + 4 Bonsplit commits, all 6 locales filled). Review phase started in sibling surface:46. Will invoke /trident-code-review against branch and apply/escalate per synthesis-action.md contract.

Review complete by agent:claude-opus-4-7-c11-10-review.

**Trident verdict:** `fix-then-merge` — no Blockers; data model and plumbing are solid across all 8 reviews; validated findings cluster at the user/API edges. (Critical-Claude reviewer hit a usage cap mid-run and did not produce its file; the other 8 reviews + 4 syntheses landed cleanly. Per trident fallback policy, the Critical-lens synthesis ran with 2 inputs instead of 3.)

**Synthesis-action artifact:** `notes/trident-review-C11-10-pack-20260504-2224/synthesis-action.md` (full review pack: standard/critical/evolutionary × claude/codex/gemini, plus four syntheses).

## Applied (Apply-by-default)

All Important + Mediums from `synthesis-action.md` landed on `c11-10-surface-tab-colors`:

- **bdb1dfe** C11-10 Review fix (I1): `c11 surface-color` targets caller's env workspace — `resolveWorkspaceColorTarget` now prefers `CMUX_WORKSPACE_ID`/`C11_WORKSPACE_ID` before falling back to focused workspace, matching the env-first pattern at CLI/c11.swift:1762 ('identify'). Fixes `not_found` errors when running from an unfocused surface.
- **9003896** C11-10 Review fix (I2): `surface-color get` fails on stale/wrong-workspace UUID — the lookup now throws `Surface not found` instead of printing `color: (none)` / `{}` for misrouted lookups. Matches v2 server semantics.
- **9521966** C11-10 Review fix (I5): `surface.set_custom_color` rejects ambiguous `clear=true` + `hex` payloads with `invalid_params` instead of silently letting clear win.
- **0ea2c18** C11-10 Review fix (M1, M2): `surface-color set` missing-arg error now hints at quoting hex strings starting with `#`; `surface-color clear` rejects trailing positional arguments.
- **269e85f** (bonsplit) + **885a520** (c11 pointer bump): C11-10 Review fixes (I3, I4) — Bonsplit's custom-color rail now respects `appearance.tabActiveIndicatorHeight` (parity with the default selected indicator); Tab Color submenu palette swatches are cached by hex via a file-private `NSCache`, eliminating N×M `lockFocus`/`setFill` calls per SwiftUI re-render. Public Bonsplit API unchanged. Bonsplit branch `c11-10-review-fixes` merged ff into bonsplit `main` on `Stage-11-Agentics/bonsplit`.

No Blockers. No Evolutionary clear wins (the strongest candidate — updating the c11 SKILL to teach color self-painting — is a skill/doc edit and belongs in a separate change).

## Escalated to delegator (Surface-to-user)

Five items were validated as real but not applied silently. Reviewer rationale + my annotation below; the delegator decides which to ticket.

- **S1: Bonsplit tab-color palette labels render as raw English in localized builds.** Built-in palette names at `Sources/TabManager.swift:250-267` and `"Custom \(index + 1)"` at `:319-322` flow into `BonsplitTabColorMenuItem` and render verbatim. Critical-codex flagged this. Pre-existing condition — the same issue exists in the workspace-color flow at `Sources/ContentView.swift:11737`. Not a C11-10 regression. *My annotation:* deserves a follow-up ticket that localizes both flows together; it is a real CLAUDE.md policy violation but applying it inside this PR introduces inconsistency unless the workspace-color flow is fixed in lockstep.
- **S2: Custom-colored tab rendering does not adjust hex for dark-mode legibility.** Plan called for "color brightened/adjusted for dark"; standard-codex notes the custom-color branch draws raw hex. Three plausible fix paths (c11 sends render-adjusted hex; Bonsplit adjusts internally; accept current behavior) — design call, not unambiguous from the reviews. *My annotation:* worth deciding before next iteration; the easiest practical fix is c11 mirroring an appearance-adjusted hex into Bonsplit while preserving the raw value in `panelCustomColors`.
- **S3: Visual marker is top-rail-only; plan called for "top accent + small leading rail/dot."** Standard-codex explicitly says "the restrained top rail may be acceptable, but it is a visible departure from the planned affordance." Product/scope decision. *My annotation:* if dense pane layouts are the target, the leading dot does help legibility — but Impl already shipped the simpler form, so this is operator preference rather than a bug.
- **S4: `WorkspaceTabColorSettings.addCustomColor` permanently grows the app-wide palette as a side effect of one tab-color pick.** Standard-claude flagged this; pre-existing pattern shared with the workspace-color flow at `Sources/ContentView.swift:12474, :12500`. Cleanest fix is a multi-flow refactor (gate `addCustomColor` behind explicit "Save to Palette"). *My annotation:* deferred for parity reasons.
- **S5: `surface.set_custom_color` returns `not_found` (Surface) when the workspace is missing, vs the surface itself.** Matches the existing pattern in `v2SurfaceFocus` (`Sources/TerminalController.swift:6177-6210`); distinct error codes (`workspace_not_found` vs `surface_not_found`) would aid SDK diagnostics. *My annotation:* tightening this for one method is stylistic; doing it across all v2 methods is a separate cleanup ticket.

## Escalated to delegator (Evolutionary worth considering)

- **E1: Update the c11 SKILL to teach agents to set their own surface tab color when spawning siblings.** All three evolutionary reviewers converged on this independently (claude L1, codex agent lanes, gemini agentic status reflection). `surface.list` and `surface.current` already expose `custom_color`; agents could read sibling colors when spawning, pick a distinct palette entry, and set their own. Highest-leverage downstream of C11-10; pure skill/doc change; not part of this PR but the lowest-cost compounding follow-up.
- **E2: Generalize `panelCustomTitles` + `panelCustomColors` (and friends) into a `PerSurfaceProperties` seam.** All three evolutionary reviewers flagged the parallel-dictionary pattern as an architectural inflection point (this is the third instance of the same 8-touchpoint scatter — snapshot capture, restore, prune, detach payload, attach restore, Bonsplit mirror, socket payload, CLI). Natural anchor sites already exist (canonical setter at `Sources/Workspace.swift:6109`, palette construction at `:8853`).
- **E3: Resolve the typed-property vs metadata namespace fork.** c11 now has two ways to write surface attributes — `surface.set_metadata` (precedence-ranked, capped, opaque) and `surface.set_custom_color` (typed, no precedence, ungated). The cheapest fix is a read-through projection: in `setPanelCustomColor`, also write `custom_color` into `SurfaceMetadataStore` so anything reading metadata sees it without learning a second API.

## Already-acknowledged deviations (from operator) — not re-flagged

- NSAlert vs PaneInteractionFeatureFlag-gated `presentTextInput` (parity with workspace-color flow).
- Slice 4 palette refresh deferred (no UserDefaults observer).
- Bonsplit pointer bump in 29ba7316 includes the chrome-scale knobs PR #4 (`8db34f0`) — incidental upstream movement.

## Branch pushed; ready for Validate.

c11 branch `c11-10-surface-tab-colors` is at `885a5209` on `origin`. Bonsplit branch `c11-10-review-fixes` is at `269e85f` on `origin`, merged ff into bonsplit `main`.

CI runs the test suite per project policy (no local test runs). Full review pack and syntheses are in `notes/trident-review-C11-10-pack-20260504-2224/`.

Tagged build: c11-10-tabcolor (`./scripts/reload.sh --tag c11-10-tabcolor`).
- First two attempts failed; build only went green after two C11-10 fix commits I landed on this branch (see "Build fixes" below).

Build fixes (committed + pushed to `c11-10-surface-tab-colors`):
- `6bae11e1` add missing `resolveCurrentWorkspaceId` helper — slice 5 (dc5dd408) referenced this helper from `runSurfaceColorSet/Clear/Get` but never defined it; cli compile failed `cannot find 'resolveCurrentWorkspaceId' in scope` ×3.
- `9ba6853d` lift `try` over `??` in `runSurfaceColorGet` — slice 5 wrote `workspaceHandle ?? (try …)` which Swift rejects when the `??` itself isn't inside a `try` chain (the sibling Set/Clear sites compile because they sit inside `try resolveSurfaceId(...)`).

Both fixes are minimal, scoped to the C11-10 surface-color CLI block, and behavior-preserving. Branch now at `9ba6853d` (was `885a5209` at Review).

Programmatic checks (CLI / socket via tagged build, non-focus):
- set/get/clear cycle: pass — `OK surface_color=#FF6B6B` → `color: #FF6B6B` → `OK surface color cleared` → `color: (none)`
- list-palette: pass — 10 entries (aurora, carbon, ember, graphite, lagoon, lilac, moss, ochre, pine, plum)
- env-first workspace fallback (Review I1): pass — `CMUX_WORKSPACE_ID=<ws2-uuid> c11 surface-color set "#4ECDC4" --surface <surf2-uuid>` (no `--workspace` flag) lands on workspace:2 only; workspace:1 unaffected
- stale-UUID error (Review I2): pass — `surface-color get --workspace ws2 --surface 00000000-...` exits 1 with `Error: Surface not found: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000`
- ambiguous clear+hex rejection (Review I5): pass — raw `surface.set_custom_color {hex:"#FF0000", clear:true, ...}` returns `{"ok":false, "error":{"code":"invalid_params","message":"'clear' and 'hex' are mutually exclusive"}}`
- surface.list / surface.current carry custom_color: pass — both v2 responses include `"custom_color":"#BADA55"` after a set
- no focus side-effect: pass — focused workspace stays on workspace:1 (`current-workspace` unchanged) across a `surface-color set` targeting workspace:2; surface-color CLI invokes only data methods (`surface.set_custom_color`, `surface.list`), never any focus-intent v2 method

Additional programmatic coverage (beyond the prompt list, since most "visual" checks have a data substrate I could exercise):
- New surface starts uncolored: pass — `surface-color get` on a freshly-split surface returns `color: (none)`
- Cross-pane move preserves color: pass — `move-surface --pane <other>` keeps `#BADA55` on the moved surface
- Reorder preserves color: pass — `reorder-surface --index 0` keeps `#BADA55`
- Cross-workspace move (detach/reattach analogue): pass — `move-surface --workspace workspace:1` then back to workspace:2 keeps `#BADA55` on both legs
- Quit + relaunch persistence: pass — `pkill` the tagged app + `open` the tagged `.app`, color survives (session restore round-trips `customColor`)

5-minute operator smoke-test:
1. From the worktree: `./scripts/reload.sh --tag c11-10-tabcolor` (incremental rebuild now that two build-fix commits have landed; xcframework already on disk).
2. Right-click any pane tab → Tab Color → pick a palette color. Confirm the tab strip shows the color rail.
3. Tab Color → Choose Custom Color… → enter `#BADA55`, Apply. Confirm rail repaints.
4. `pkill -f "c11 DEV c11-10-tabcolor.app/Contents/MacOS/c11" && open "/Users/atin/Library/Developer/Xcode/DerivedData/c11-c11-10-tabcolor/Build/Products/Debug/c11 DEV c11-10-tabcolor.app"` — confirm the color persists after relaunch.
5. From any terminal in the tagged build: `C11_SOCKET=/tmp/c11-debug-c11-10-tabcolor.sock /tmp/c11-cli surface-color get` (operates on the focused tab) — confirm value matches the UI rail.

Issues found:
- Two build-blocker compile errors in slice 5 (dc5dd408) — fixed and pushed (see Build fixes above). Pre-existing Review trident did not exercise the build, so these slipped through.
- One environment hiccup that needed manual repair before the first build could even start: the worktree's `ghostty` submodule was missing `vendor/glad/src/gl.c` (and the submodule index was empty — `git ls-files` returned nothing while the working tree had a partial checkout). Resolved by `cd ghostty && git restore --source=HEAD --staged --worktree -- vendor`. Also removed a stale `index.lock` (zero bytes, no live git pid). This is environmental, not C11-10 code; flagging in case the worktree was prepared by an earlier agent that didn't finish initializing submodules.
